content.full_text:
July 30, 1990

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard, Division Chief  
California Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

This letter concerns disclosure requirements for candidates appearing on slate cards.

We have been advised that each candidate who purchased a position on a slate card must attach a copy of Form 401, Schedule B to his own campaign report Form 490.

I would respectfully request that you reconsider this advice, as such a requirement would be unnecessarily burdensome without any compensating improvement in public disclosure. Since slate card organizations are already required to file Form 401, the public has ready access to all information regarding each slate card. Requiring each candidate to wait for receipt of Form 401, Schedule B, from slate card organizations places each candidate in danger of filing form 490 late, and suffering corresponding penalties. At the very least, candidates would be required to file expensive and burdensome amendments for reasons beyond their control.

If I may, I would like to suggest that candidates instead be required to provide on Form 490 the slate card organization’s FPPC number in addition to the slate organization name and amount expended. This would expedite disclosure of slate card information to interested parties without jeopardizing candidate compliance with filing deadlines.

I look forward to hearing from you. Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,

Eileen E. Padberg

Eileen E. Padberg  
2081 BUSINESS CENTER DRIVE, SUITE 125  
IRVINE, CALIFORNIA 92715  
(714) 756-8477   FAX: (714) 756-2215
